requirement_en: Given pointers to two integers, swap their stored values in place.
Given pointers to two integers, set the first value to 0 only if the second value is nonzero, otherwise leave both unchanged.
Given pointers to three integers, reorder the values so they are in nondecreasing order.
Given pointers to two integers, return the larger of the two pointed values without modifying memory.
Given pointers to integers a and b, increase a by b and return the updated value of a.
Given unsigned x and nonzero unsigned y, store quotient and remainder of x divided by y through output pointers.
Given pointers to three integers, return the sum of the three pointed values.
Given pointers to two integers, return the sum of the two pointed values.
Given an integer array and its length, sort the array in nondecreasing order in place.
Given an integer array and its length, replace each element with double its original value.
Given an integer array and its length, reverse the element order in place.
Given an integer array and its length, set elements at even indices to 0 and leave odd indices unchanged.
Given two integer arrays and a length, return 1 if all compared elements are equal, otherwise return 0.
Given two integers, return the larger value.
Given an integer array, its length, and a constant c, add c to every array element in place.
Given an integer array and two valid indices, swap the elements at those indices.
Given a nonempty integer array and its length, return the maximum element.
Given an integer array, its length, and a target value, return the first index of the target or -1 if absent.
Given a nonnegative integer n, return the sum of all even numbers from 0 to n inclusive.
Given a nonnegative integer, return the sum of its decimal digits.
Given integers a and b with a nonnegative, return their product using repeated addition.
Given a nonnegative integer n, return n factorial.
Given a nonnegative integer n, return the sum of powers of two from 2^0 through 2^n.
Given a positive integer c, return c by counting loop iterations while decrementing from c to 0.
Given a nonnegative integer n, return the arithmetic sum of integers from 0 to n inclusive.
Run a loop that increments a counter from 0 until it reaches 30, yielding final value 30.
Given an integer array, target value, and length, return 1 if the target exists in the array, else 0.
Given an integer array, target value, and length, perform linear search and return 1 if found, otherwise 0.
Given nonnegative x and positive y, return the integer quotient of x divided by y using repeated subtraction.
Given an array, length, and value x, return the number of occurrences of x and output their total sum through a pointer.
Given a nonempty integer array and length, return the maximum array value.
Given an integer array and length, return 1 if every element is even, otherwise return 0.
Given a sorted integer array, target value, and length, return the target index via binary search or -1 if not found.
Given an integer array and length, return the sum of its elements.
Given integers x and y, adjust y by +10 and x by -5, then return their sum.
Given three side lengths, return 1 if they satisfy triangle inequality, else return 0.
Given three angles, return 1 if all are positive and sum to 180, otherwise return 0.
Given pointers to two integers, swap their values.
Given principal, time, and rate, return simple interest computed as p*n*r/100.
Given integer x and nonnegative exponent n, return x raised to the power n.
Given two integers, return the greater one (or either when equal).
Given two nonnegative integers, return their greatest common divisor.
Given integers x and y, return the difference x minus y.
Given integer n, return 1 plus the count of terms in the sequence 7,10,13,... that are less than or equal to n.
Given two integers, return their sum.
Given an integer, return its absolute value.
Given x, y, and an output pointer r with y positive, return quotient and store remainder such that x = quotient*y + remainder and remainder < y.
Given a nonnegative integer x, return x by decrementing a counter to zero and counting iterations.
Given a nonzero integer selector a, choose fixed values and return 10 divided by their sum (which evaluates to 1 for nonzero a).
Given a nonempty integer array and its length, return the maximum element.
Given base and height, return the integer area of a triangle computed as base*height/2.
